zotero.abstractNote: Curiosity is a fundamental driver of learning, yet the mechanisms by which it guides exploration remain debated. We investigate how world models—internal representations of environmental dynamics—interact with curiosity-driven exploration. We propose a framework where agents use world models to predict future states, and curiosity is modulated by the model's predictive uncertainty. Through a series of experiments, we demonstrate that this interaction leads to a transition from broad, novelty-seeking exploration to targeted, competence-building behavior. Our results suggest that the development of a robust world model is essential for shifting from curiosity to competence, providing a computational account of how agents balance exploration and exploitation in complex environments.
zotero.conferenceName: 47th Annual Conference of the Cognitive Science Society
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 47th Annual Conference of the Cognitive Science Society
